name: axum
what_it_does: Ergonomic and modular Rust web framework built on top of Tokio and Tower. axum uses Tower middleware for composable HTTP handling, with compile-time type-checked extractors for request parsing. Part of the Tokio ecosystem alongside reqwest and tokio-postgres. Known for exceptional performance, memory safety, and zero-cost abstractions for building high-performance agent HTTP servers in Rust.
use_cases: Build Rust-based agent API servers with compile-time type safety and Tower middleware composability
Create high-performance agent microservices with Rust's memory safety and zero-cost async via Tokio
Implement WebSocket and SSE endpoints for real-time agent streaming in Rust backends
Build agent services that share Tower middleware layers with reqwest client calls for consistent observability
Deploy agent APIs as statically compiled Rust binaries with near-zero overhead HTTP handling
not_for: Non-Rust projects — axum requires Rust; use FastAPI (Python) or Gin (Go) for those languages
Teams without Rust experience — Rust's learning curve is significant; don't start here
Rapid prototyping — Rust compilation and type system increase development time vs Python/Go
best_when: You're building a high-performance Rust agent service where compile-time correctness, memory safety, and minimal overhead are priorities.
avoid_when: You're not using Rust or need rapid prototyping — use FastAPI or Gin for faster iteration.

auth.notes: No built-in auth — implement via Tower middleware layers. Common: tower-http Bearer token or custom extractor.

pricing.notes: Completely free and open source, maintained by the Tokio team.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — runs on your infrastructure

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Compile-time errors via Rust type system catch many mistakes before runtime. IntoResponse trait for custom error types. Tower service errors propagate with type information.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: HTTP handler idempotency is application responsibility. Rust's type system enforces correct state management.
known_agent_gotchas: Handler type constraints are complex — axum uses blanket impl magic; when handler doesn't compile, error messages from Rust trait bounds can be intimidating
State sharing requires Arc<T> wrapping — shared mutable state must use Arc<Mutex<T>> or Arc<RwLock<T>>; axum's State extractor clones the Arc cheaply
Extractors are applied in order and consume the request body — only one body extractor (Json, Bytes, String) can be used per handler
axum 0.6 → 0.7 had significant breaking changes — check migration guide when upgrading; patterns for routing and middleware changed
Tower middleware (tower-http) is the extension mechanism — understanding Tower's Service trait is necessary for advanced middleware composition
WebSocket upgrade consumes the connection — code after ws.on_upgrade() is not reachable; all WebSocket logic goes in the callback

security_score_components.security_notes: Rust memory safety eliminates entire classes of vulnerabilities (buffer overflow, use-after-free). TLS via rustls or native-tls. Type system prevents many injection vulnerabilities.
